text:
In visual arts, you had a sandbox.

You did not wait until you were ready. You made something. You looked at it. You made it again. The feedback was immediate and the stakes were low and that is exactly how you got good.

I drew the same hand hundreds of times before anyone depended on it. Each attempt was just information. Wrong proportions. Fix it. Wrong shadow. Fix it. The wrongness was the lesson.

Nobody handed me a textbook about hands. They handed me paper and said go.

That is how mastery works. Not in the knowing. In the doing it again.
